title: mentha citrata subsp. pubescens
intro: Mentha citrata subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy bergamot mint, is a subspecies of the bergamot mint plant, which belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of citronellol and limonene, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Mentha citrata subsp. pubescens include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve stress and anxiety, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
